rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies verifying whether the proposed architecture, stability claim, switching mechanism, and conclusions form a consistent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Because the section reports experimental outcomes and draws claims from them, review centrally includes checking claim support against the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Reviewing a section commonly includes assessing section-level ordering and whether the narrative progresses coherently from motivation to method to evidence to conclusion.
rationale.R1.6: A section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection must account for uncertainty about what can and cannot be determined from the provided excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad deployment and transfer claims, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than performing the requested review or editing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns robot teleoperation and stability guarantees, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from unsafe or overstated technical claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit content.
